answer_text: Charminar and Yakutpura show the most extreme land-surface temperature in 2026 — Charminar tops the city at 84, driven by dense built-up cover and very low vegetation. Musheerabad, Malakpet and LB Nagar follow close behind. I recommend a cool-roof subsidy for the top heat wards and an aggressive ward-level tree-cover target.
actions: Launch a cool-roof subsidy for the top-5 heat wards
Set a +15% ward tree-cover target by 2028
Convert 3 vacant plots per ward into micro-parks
Add misting/shade structures at high-footfall markets in Charminar
